sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.33 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: b) Let$G(q)$be a simple Chevalley group over a field of order$q$. Prove that there exists$m$such that the restriction of every non-one-dimensional representation of$G(q^m)$over a field of prime characteristic not dividing$q$to$G(q)$contains all irreducible representations of$G(q)$as composition factors. }

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
